message:
feat(wizard): add personalized command builder panel (bd-31ps.4)

Adds a command builder that generates personalized SSH, installer, and
post-install commands based on user preferences (IP, OS, mode, username,
git ref). Integrated into the launch-onboarding wizard page.

- New: commandBuilder.ts with buildCommands() and buildShareURL()
- New: CommandBuilderPanel component with IP input, mode toggle,
  advanced settings, and copy-to-clipboard for each command
- Extended userPreferences.ts with InstallMode, SSH username, and
  ACFS git ref preferences (localStorage + URL params)
- All quality gates pass (type-check, lint, build)

Closes bd-31ps.4.1 and bd-31ps.4.2.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-31ps.2","title":"Add acfs support-bundle command (diagnostics pack)","description":"## Background
Support/debugging requires scattered manual steps (logs, state.json, doctor output, versions). A single support bundle simplifies triage and reduces back-and-forth.

## Goal
Add an `acfs support-bundle` command that collects diagnostics into a timestamped archive with redaction.

## Scope
- New script in scripts/lib/support.sh.
- New CLI entrypoint via acfs command dispatcher.
- Bundle includes: state.json, install logs, doctor --json, versions, OS info, selected config files, recent errors.

## Considerations
- Redact tokens/secrets (API keys, auth tokens).
- Size limits and opt-in for large logs.
- Output should be safe to share.

## Testing
- Unit tests for redaction + file collection.
- Docker E2E: create bundle and verify contents.

## Acceptance Criteria
- One command produces a shareable bundle with manifest + logs + doctor JSON.
- Redaction verified and documented.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:22:17.754579012Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T23:22:17.754579012Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-31ps.2","depends_on_id":"bd-31ps","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:22:17.754579012Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-31ps.2.1","title":"Support-bundle implementation + CLI wiring","description":"## Goal
Implement the support-bundle collection logic and CLI entrypoint.

## Tasks
- Create scripts/lib/support.sh with functions: collect_files, capture_doctor_json, capture_versions, capture_env_summary.
- Add `acfs support-bundle` command routing (acfs wrapper / doctor.sh dispatch table).
- Output bundle directory: ~/.acfs/support/<timestamp>/ plus .tar.gz archive.
- Include a manifest JSON describing included files and versions.

## Considerations
- Ensure stdout stays clean for piping; write progress to stderr via logging.sh.
- Avoid requiring root; use sudo only when needed for logs.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Running `acfs support-bundle` produces a tarball + manifest JSON.
- Bundle includes doctor JSON and state.json when present.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:22:37.265721256Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-26T22:40:31.376687322Z","closed_at":"2026-01-26T22:40:31.376618843Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-31ps.2.1","depends_on_id":"bd-31ps.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:22:37.265721256Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-31ps.2.1","depends_on_id":"bd-31ps.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:41:52.853108258Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-31ps.2.1","depends_on_id":"bd-31ps.3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:42:22.564866490Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-31ps.2.2","title":"Support-bundle redaction rules + tests","description":"## Goal
Define and implement redaction rules for the support bundle.

## Tasks
- Identify sensitive patterns: API keys, auth tokens, bearer headers, .env secrets, OAuth refresh tokens.
- Implement redaction in support.sh (regex-based, with allowlist for safe values).
- Provide a summary report of redactions performed.
- Add a `--no-redact` flag for advanced users (with warning).

## Tests
- Unit tests: known secrets replaced with <REDACTED>.
- Ensure false positives are minimal (e.g., Git SHAs not redacted).

## Acceptance Criteria
- Bundle safe to share by default.
- Redaction summary included in manifest JSON.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:22:55.389196201Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-26T22:46:58.482794507Z","closed_at":"2026-01-26T22:46:58.482772185Z","close_reason":"Implemented redaction rules: API keys (sk-*), AWS keys (AKIA*), GitHub tokens (ghp_/ghs_/github_pat_), Vault tokens (hvs.*), Slack tokens (xox*), Bearer tokens, JWTs, and generic key=value secrets. Added --no-redact flag, redaction summary in manifest JSON.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-31ps.2.2","depends_on_id":"bd-31ps.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:22:55.389196201Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-31ps.2.3","title":"Tests: acfs support-bundle","description":"## Goal
Add automated coverage for support-bundle generation.

## Tasks
- Unit tests for collection functions (mock files + redaction).
- Docker E2E: run `acfs support-bundle`, verify tarball contains expected files + manifest JSON.
- Log output to /tmp/acfs_support_bundle_test_TIMESTAMP.log with pass/fail summary.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Tests fail if required bundle components are missing.
- E2E run produces detailed logs + summary.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:23:05.075169407Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T23:42:51.658165458Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-31ps.2.3","depends_on_id":"bd-31ps.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:23:05.075169407Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-31ps.2.3","depends_on_id":"bd-31ps.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:42:39.178930848Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-31ps.2.3","depends_on_id":"bd-31ps.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:42:51.657404163Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-31ps.2.4","title":"Docs: support-bundle usage + privacy","description":"## Goal
Document support-bundle usage and privacy expectations.

## Tasks
- Add README section describing `acfs support-bundle` and where output is stored.
- Include redaction notes + how to disable with --no-redact.
- Add troubleshooting guidance on what to attach when filing issues.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Users can find and run the command easily.
- Clear warning about sensitive data and redaction behavior.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:23:22.919954847Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T23:23:22.919954847Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-31ps.2.4","depends_on_id":"bd-31ps.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:23:22.919954847Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-31ps.3","title":"Structured install logging + JSON summary artifacts","description":"## Background
Install failures are hard to diagnose without durable logs. We want deterministic artifacts: a log file and a JSON summary of phases, durations, and failures.

## Goal
Add structured install logging (log file + JSON summary) without polluting stdout.

## Scope
- Default log file under ~/.acfs/logs/install_YYYYmmdd_HHMMSS.log.
- JSON summary artifact with phase timings, failed step, and resume hints.
- Redaction for sensitive values in logs.

## Considerations
- Respect --quiet and --dry-run behaviors.
- Preserve stderr progress output.

## Testing
- Unit tests for summary JSON and redaction.
- Integration test in tests/vm to assert artifacts exist.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Every install run produces a log file and summary JSON.
- Summary is stable and parseable for tooling.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:23:49.243580242Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T23:23:49.243580242Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-31ps.3","depends_on_id":"bd-31ps","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:23:49.243580242Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-31ps.3.1","title":"Installer log file capture (stderr tee)","description":"## Goal
Implement install log file capture with minimal changes to existing logging output.

## Tasks
- Add log file support in scripts/lib/logging.sh (tee stderr to file when enabled).
- In install.sh, initialize log path early and export ACFS_LOG_FILE.
- Ensure log file creation respects TARGET_HOME and permissions.

## Considerations
- Do not break stdout cleanliness.
- Preserve --quiet behavior (errors still logged).

## Acceptance Criteria
- Install logs are written to ~/.acfs/logs/*.log by default.
- Logs include all phase headers and warnings.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:24:45.032818846Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-26T22:31:14.206012790Z","closed_at":"2026-01-26T22:31:14.205945734Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-31ps.3.1","depends_on_id":"bd-31ps.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:24:45.032818846Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-31ps.3.2","title":"Installer JSON summary schema + emission","description":"## Goal
Define and emit a stable JSON summary for install runs.

## Tasks
- Define schema: phases (start/end/duration), failed_phase, failed_step, resume_hint, environment (ubuntu version, mode).
- Emit summary JSON at end of install (and on failure) to ~/.acfs/logs/install_summary_<timestamp>.json.
- Ensure summary includes pointer to log file path.

## Tests
- Unit test JSON schema generation with sample data.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Summary JSON is produced for success and failure paths.
- Output is stable for downstream tooling.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:25:05.047426690Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-26T22:36:09.415113719Z","closed_at":"2026-01-26T22:36:09.415046031Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-31ps.3.2","depends_on_id":"bd-31ps.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:25:05.047426690Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-31ps.3.3","title":"Tests: install log + summary artifacts","description":"## Goal
Add automated validation for install logs and summary artifacts.

## Tasks
- Extend tests/vm/test_install_ubuntu.sh or add new VM test to assert log + summary JSON exist.
- Verify JSON schema fields are present and parseable.
- Log test output to /tmp/acfs_install_log_test_TIMESTAMP.log with summary.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Tests fail if artifacts are missing or malformed.
- Log includes paths to artifacts for debugging.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:25:31.639922610Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T23:43:33.604190266Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-31ps.3.3","depends_on_id":"bd-31ps.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:25:31.639922610Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-31ps.3.3","depends_on_id":"bd-31ps.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:43:23.928393439Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-31ps.3.3","depends_on_id":"bd-31ps.3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:43:33.603581980Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-31ps.3.4","title":"Docs: install log + summary JSON","description":"## Goal
Document install log location, summary JSON fields, and how to share logs.

## Tasks
- Update README install troubleshooting section with log paths.
- Add a brief JSON schema snippet to docs.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Users can find logs and understand summary fields.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:25:46.240700942Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T23:25:46.240700942Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-31ps.3.4","depends_on_id":"bd-31ps.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:25:46.240700942Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-31ps.4","title":"Wizard command builder (personalized + shareable)","description":"## Background
Beginners frequently copy the wrong command or forget flags (mode/ref/username). A guided command builder removes error-prone manual edits.

## Goal
Add a wizard-based command builder that outputs personalized SSH + install + verify commands, with shareable URL state.

## Scope
- Inputs: IP/hostname, username, mode (vibe/safe), optional ACFS_REF pin.
- Outputs: SSH command, installer command, post-install checks.
- Persist via localStorage and URL query params.

## Considerations
- No backend.
- Must be mobile-friendly with copy buttons.

## Testing
- Playwright E2E for persistence + copy outputs.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Users can copy a correct, personalized one-liner.
- State persists across refresh and shareable links.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:25:59.566341232Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T23:25:59.566341232Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-31ps.4","depends_on_id":"bd-31ps","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:25:59.566341232Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-31ps.4.1","title":"Wizard command builder: UX spec + data model","description":"## Goal
Define the command builder UX and data model.

## Tasks
- Decide which wizard steps surface inputs (IP/user/mode/ref).
- Define URL param schema (e.g., ?ip=, ?user=, ?mode=, ?ref=).
- Draft command templates for SSH, installer, doctor, onboard.

## Considerations
- Ensure commands match README + install.sh flags.
- Validate input (basic IP/hostname pattern).

## Acceptance Criteria
- Clear UX spec and data model ready for implementation.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:26:19.925128674Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-26T22:41:16.150116705Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-31ps.4.1","depends_on_id":"bd-31ps.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:26:19.925128674Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-31ps.4.4","title":"Analytics: command builder copy events","description":"## Goal
Add lightweight analytics for command builder usage to understand adoption.

## Tasks
- Emit GA events for copy actions (ssh/install/doctor).
- Ensure events only fire when analytics is configured.

## Acceptance Criteria
- No runtime errors when GA is disabled.
- Events include basic context (command type).","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:27:09.180357135Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T23:27:09.180357135Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-31ps.4.4","depends_on_id":"bd-31ps.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:27:09.180357135Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-31ps.5","title":"Doctor uses generated checks + fix suggestions","description":"## Background
Doctor checks can drift from manifest install/verify logic. The manifest already defines verify commands; doctor should reuse them and add module-specific fix hints.

## Goal
Make acfs doctor consume generated checks and include actionable per-module fix suggestions (e.g., acfs install --only <module>).

## Scope
- Source scripts/generated/doctor_checks.sh in doctor.sh.
- Add mapping from module id -> fix suggestion and category.
- Preserve existing bespoke checks (DCG hook, PATH, etc.).

## Testing
- Unit tests for suggestion output.
- VM test to ensure doctor output is stable.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Doctor output matches manifest verify commands.
- Fix hints are shown for missing modules.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:27:26.961154284Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T23:27:26.961154284Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-31ps.5","depends_on_id":"bd-31ps","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:27:26.961154284Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-pkta","title":"Profile full installer runtime in Docker (baseline + hotspots)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T19:00:21.329191150Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-22T01:25:55.941958502Z","closed_at":"2026-01-22T01:25:55.940359722Z","close_reason":"Profiling completed via state.json analysis. Baseline: 1078s total. Hotspots identified: (1) cli_tools 455s/42.2% - apt packages, GitHub releases, lazygit/lazydocker builds; (2) languages 372s/34.5% - rust/cargo compilation (batched cargo install already implemented in bd-3vx8); (3) stack 96s/8.9% - Dicklesworthstone tool downloads. Optimization priority: cli_tools phase which has most room for parallelization of apt operations and binary downloads.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-pkta","depends_on_id":"bd-2z32","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"2026-01-21T19:00:21.367573510Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-td8y","title":"Deep exploration: Mail (MCP Agent Mail)","description":"## Goal
Perform deep exploration of MCP Agent Mail and revise its description on the flywheel/TLDR pages with comprehensive testing and validation.

## Phase 0: Pre-flight Verification (CRITICAL)

### 0.1 Tool Existence Check
```bash
#!/bin/bash
LOG=/tmp/mail-preflight.log
echo "=== Mail Pre-flight Check: $(date) ===" | tee $LOG

# Verify tool directory exists
if [[ -d /dp/mcp_agent_mail ]]; then
  echo "PASS: /dp/mcp_agent_mail directory exists" | tee -a $LOG
else
  echo "FAIL: /dp/mcp_agent_mail NOT FOUND - aborting" | tee -a $LOG
  exit 1
fi

# Verify README exists
[[ -f /dp/mcp_agent_mail/README.md ]] && echo "PASS: README.md exists" || echo "WARN: README.md not found"

# Check if MCP server is configured
grep -r "mcp-agent-mail" ~/.claude/settings.json 2>/dev/null && echo "PASS: MCP server configured" || echo "WARN: MCP server may not be configured"
```

### 0.2 Content Snapshot (BEFORE State)
```bash
#!/bin/bash
SNAPSHOT_DIR=/tmp/mail-exploration-snapshots
mkdir -p $SNAPSHOT_DIR
cp /data/projects/agentic_coding_flywheel_setup/apps/web/lib/flywheel.ts $SNAPSHOT_DIR/flywheel.ts.before
cp /data/projects/agentic_coding_flywheel_setup/apps/web/lib/tldr-content.ts $SNAPSHOT_DIR/tldr-content.ts.before
grep -A 100 "id: 'mail'" /data/projects/agentic_coding_flywheel_setup/apps/web/lib/flywheel.ts | head -100 > $SNAPSHOT_DIR/mail-flywheel-entry.before.txt
echo "Snapshots saved to $SNAPSHOT_DIR"
```

### 0.3 Verify TypeScript Interfaces
```bash
grep -A 30 "export interface FlywheelTool" /data/projects/agentic_coding_flywheel_setup/apps/web/lib/flywheel.ts
grep -A 30 "export interface TldrFlywheelTool" /data/projects/agentic_coding_flywheel_setup/apps/web/lib/tldr-content.ts
```

## Phase 1: Research (GATHER CONTEXT)

### 1.1 Primary Documentation
```bash
wc -l /dp/mcp_agent_mail/README.md
cat /dp/mcp_agent_mail/README.md
ls -la /dp/mcp_agent_mail/docs/ 2>/dev/null
cat /dp/mcp_agent_mail/AGENTS.md 2>/dev/null
```

### 1.2 Code Investigation
- Launch code investigation agent to understand:
  - FastMCP server architecture and tool registration
  - SQLite storage schema (messages, agents, file_reservations, agent_links)
  - Advisory file reservation system (exclusive vs shared, TTL, force release)
  - Message threading, FTS5 search, summarization
  - All MCP tools: send_message, reply_message, fetch_inbox, mark_message_read, acknowledge_message
  - File reservation tools: file_reservation_paths, release_file_reservations, renew_file_reservations
  - Agent tools: register_agent, create_agent_identity, whois
  - Macro helpers: macro_start_session, macro_prepare_thread, macro_file_reservation_cycle
  - Git archive persistence layer

### 1.3 External Context Search
```bash
/xf search 'agent mail OR mcp_agent_mail OR file_reservation' 2>&1 | head -50
cass search 'agent mail mcp coordination' --robot --limit 10 2>&1
cass search 'file reservation lock' --robot --limit 5 2>&1
```

### 1.4 Project State Review
```bash
ls -la /dp/mcp_agent_mail/.beads/ 2>/dev/null
cd /dp/mcp_agent_mail && git log --oneline -20 && cd -
find /dp/mcp_agent_mail -name "*.md" -type f 2>/dev/null | head -10
```

### 1.5 MCP Tool Inventory
```bash
# List actual MCP tools from source
grep -r "def.*(" /dp/mcp_agent_mail/src/*.py 2>/dev/null | grep -v "__" | head -30
# Or check the tool registrations
grep -r "@mcp\|tool_name\|register" /dp/mcp_agent_mail/src/*.py 2>/dev/null | head -30
```

## Phase 2: Analysis (SYNTHESIZE UNDERSTANDING)

Document findings:
- [ ] Core messaging: send_message, reply_message, fetch_inbox verified
- [ ] Agent identity: register_agent, whois verified
- [ ] File reservations: exclusive/shared modes, TTL, force release verified
- [ ] Threading: thread_id handling verified
- [ ] Search: FTS5 query syntax verified
- [ ] Macros: macro_start_session use cases verified
- [ ] Synergies VERIFIED:
  - [ ] bv: [does bv read mail messages?]
  - [ ] cm: [does cm use mail?]
  - [ ] slb: [how does slb use mail?]
  - [ ] ntm: [how does ntm coordinate via mail?]
- [ ] Tech stack verified: Python, FastMCP, SQLite, Pydantic
- [ ] Known limitations discovered

## Phase 3: Revision (UPDATE DESCRIPTIONS)

### 3.1 Update apps/web/lib/flywheel.ts
```typescript
{
  id: 'mail',
  name: 'MCP Agent Mail',
  shortName: 'Mail',
  href: string,  // valid GitHub URL
  icon: LucideIcon,
  color: string,
  tagline: string,  // e.g., "Message-passing coordination for multi-agent workflows"
  description: string,
  deepDescription: string,  // include FastMCP, SQLite, file reservations
  connectsTo: string[],  // VERIFIED synergies only
  connectionDescriptions: Record<string, string>,
  stars: number,
  features: string[],  // VERIFIED: 20+ MCP tools, file reservations, threading
  cliCommands: [
    { command: 'mcp__mcp-agent-mail__send_message', description: '...' },
    { command: 'mcp__mcp-agent-mail__file_reservation_paths', description: '...' },
    // ... all VERIFIED tools
  ],
  installCommand: string,
  language: 'Python',
}
```

### 3.2 Update apps/web/lib/tldr-content.ts
- Verify all fields match TldrFlywheelTool interface
- whatItDoes: accurate messaging + file reservation description
- synergies: VERIFIED connections only

### 3.3 Cross-reference Validation
```bash
# Verify reciprocal synergies
for tool in bv cm slb ntm; do
  if grep -A10 "id: '$tool'" /data/projects/agentic_coding_flywheel_setup/apps/web/lib/flywheel.ts | grep -q "mail"; then
    echo "PASS: $tool lists mail"
  else
    echo "WARN: $tool does NOT list mail"
  fi
done
```

### 3.4 De-slopify
- Remove: "harness", "empower", "leverage", "robust", "seamless"
- Verify no placeholder text remains

## Phase 4: Testing (VERIFY QUALITY)

### 4.1 Static Analysis
```bash
#!/bin/bash
LOG=/tmp/mail-static-analysis.log
cd /data/projects/agentic_coding_flywheel_setup/apps/web
echo "=== Mail Static Analysis ===" | tee $LOG
bun run type-check 2>&1 | tee -a $LOG; echo "Type Check: $?" | tee -a $LOG
bun run lint 2>&1 | tee -a $LOG; echo "Lint: $?" | tee -a $LOG
```

### 4.2 Build Verification
```bash
#!/bin/bash
LOG=/tmp/mail-build.log
cd /data/projects/agentic_coding_flywheel_setup/apps/web
bun run build 2>&1 | tee $LOG
if [[ $? -ne 0 ]]; then
  echo "FAIL: Build failed - restoring snapshot"
  cp /tmp/mail-exploration-snapshots/flywheel.ts.before lib/flywheel.ts
  cp /tmp/mail-exploration-snapshots/tldr-content.ts.before lib/tldr-content.ts
  exit 1
fi
```

### 4.3 E2E Visual Verification
```bash
#!/bin/bash
LOG=/tmp/mail-e2e.log
cd /data/projects/agentic_coding_flywheel_setup/apps/web

# Kill any existing dev server
lsof -t -i :3000 | xargs kill 2>/dev/null; sleep 2

bun run dev &
DEV_PID=$!
for i in {1..30}; do curl -s http://localhost:3000 &>/dev/null && break; sleep 1; done

curl -sL http://localhost:3000/flywheel | grep -qi 'mail' && echo "PASS: mail on /flywheel" || echo "FAIL"
curl -sL http://localhost:3000/tldr | grep -qi 'mail' && echo "PASS: mail on /tldr" || echo "FAIL"

kill $DEV_PID 2>/dev/null
```

### 4.4 Unit Tests: Content Integrity
```bash
#!/bin/bash
LOG=/tmp/mail-unit-tests.log
cd /data/projects/agentic_coding_flywheel_setup/apps/web
echo "=== Mail Content Tests ===" | tee $LOG

grep -q "id: 'mail'" lib/flywheel.ts && echo "PASS: mail entry exists" || echo "FAIL"
grep -q "send_message\|file_reservation" lib/flywheel.ts && echo "PASS: MCP tools mentioned" || echo "FAIL"
grep -q "id: 'mail'" lib/tldr-content.ts && echo "PASS: mail in tldr" || echo "FAIL"

# Verify content changed
diff -q lib/flywheel.ts /tmp/mail-exploration-snapshots/flywheel.ts.before && echo "WARN: No changes" || echo "PASS: Content updated"
```

### 4.5 MCP Tool Verification
```bash
# Verify documented MCP tools actually exist by checking if they're available
# This requires the MCP server to be running
echo "MCP tools to verify:"
echo "- send_message"
echo "- reply_message"  
echo "- file_reservation_paths"
echo "- release_file_reservations"
# Manual verification required with actual MCP calls
```

### 4.6 Consolidated Test Results
```bash
#!/bin/bash
RESULTS=/tmp/mail-exploration-test-results.log
echo "=== MAIL DEEP EXPLORATION RESULTS ===" > $RESULTS
echo "Date: $(date)" >> $RESULTS
grep -E "PASS|FAIL|WARN" /tmp/mail-*.log >> $RESULTS 2>/dev/null
FAIL_COUNT=$(grep -c "FAIL" $RESULTS)
echo "FAILURES: $FAIL_COUNT" >> $RESULTS
cat $RESULTS
```

## Phase 5: Commit (FINALIZE)

### 5.1 Pre-commit Check
```bash
[[ $(grep -c "FAIL" /tmp/mail-exploration-test-results.log) -gt 0 ]] && echo "ABORT: Failures" && exit 1
```

### 5.2 Commit
```bash
cd /data/projects/agentic_coding_flywheel_setup
git add apps/web/lib/flywheel.ts apps/web/lib/tldr-content.ts
git commit -m "docs(flywheel): update Agent Mail descriptions with verified MCP tools

- Verified 20+ MCP tools against actual implementation
- Documented file reservation system accurately
- Verified synergies (not assumed)
- Passed all tests

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>"

br update bd-td8y --status closed
br sync --flush-only && git add .beads/
git push
```

### 5.3 Rollback if needed
```bash
cp /tmp/mail-exploration-snapshots/*.before apps/web/lib/
```

- [ ] Pre-flight passed (tool exists)
- [ ] Content snapshot captured
- [ ] All tests PASS (zero FAIL)
- [ ] MCP tools VERIFIED against implementation
- [ ] File reservation system accurately documented
- [ ] Synergies VERIFIED (not assumed)
- [ ] Reciprocal synergies confirmed
- [ ] Content de-slopified
- [ ] Pushed to remote
- [ ] Bead closed

-{"id":"bd-w8fx","title":"VPS provider comparison table in web wizard","description":"## Overview
Add a VPS provider comparison table in the web wizard to help beginners choose between Hetzner, DigitalOcean, Vultr, etc.

## Current Problem
- Users must research providers externally
- No guidance on which provider fits their needs
- Price/performance tradeoffs unclear
- "Which one should I pick?" is common question

## Proposed Comparison Table
| Provider | Cheapest VPS | RAM | Storage | Best For |
|----------|-------------|-----|---------|----------|
| Hetzner | $4.50/mo | 4GB | 40GB | Best value |
| DigitalOcean | $6/mo | 1GB | 25GB | Simplicity |
| Vultr | $5/mo | 1GB | 25GB | Global locations |
| Linode | $5/mo | 1GB | 25GB | Good support |

## Data to Show
- Starting price (monthly)
- Specs at price point (RAM, CPU, storage)
- Data center locations
- Key differentiator
- Referral link (if applicable)

## Implementation Details
1. Create `VPSComparison` component
2. Store provider data in `lib/vpsProviders.ts`
3. Sortable/filterable table
4. "Recommended" badge for Hetzner (best value)
5. External links open in new tab

## Affiliate Disclosure
- Clear disclosure if referral links used
- Option to use direct links instead
- Transparency builds trust

## Keep Data Fresh
- Provider data in separate config file
- Easy to update prices without code changes
- Last updated timestamp shown

## Test Plan
- [ ] Test table renders all providers
- [ ] Test sorting by price, RAM, etc.
- [ ] Test external links work
- [ ] Test mobile responsive layout
- [ ] Accessibility: screen reader compatible

## Files to Create
- apps/web/components/wizard/VPSComparison.tsx
- apps/web/lib/vpsProviders.ts (provider data)

 {"id":"bd-w8iz","title":"Deep exploration: CM (CASS Memory System)","description":"## Goal
Perform deep exploration of CM (CASS Memory System) and revise its description on the flywheel/TLDR pages with comprehensive testing.

## Phase 0: Pre-flight Verification (CRITICAL)

### 0.1 Tool Existence Check
```bash
# Verify CM installation
[[ -d /dp/cass_memory_system ]] && echo "PASS: cm repo exists" || { echo "FAIL: cm repo missing"; exit 1; }
command -v cm &>/dev/null && echo "PASS: cm command available" || { echo "FAIL: cm not in PATH"; exit 1; }

# Verify cass dependency (cm depends on cass)
command -v cass &>/dev/null && echo "PASS: cass available (dependency)" || echo "WARN: cass not installed"
```

### 0.2 Content Snapshot (BEFORE State)
```bash
SNAPSHOT_DIR=/tmp/cm-exploration-snapshots-$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S)
mkdir -p $SNAPSHOT_DIR
cp apps/web/lib/flywheel.ts $SNAPSHOT_DIR/flywheel.ts.before
cp apps/web/lib/tldr-content.ts $SNAPSHOT_DIR/tldr-content.ts.before
echo "Snapshots saved to $SNAPSHOT_DIR"
```

### 0.3 TypeScript Interface Reference
Content must match FlywheelTool interface:
- id, name, tagline, description, deepDescription
- features[], cliCommands[], connectsTo[]
- integrationLevel, category, status

## Phase 1: Research (GATHER CONTEXT)

### 1.1 Primary Documentation
- `cat /dp/cass_memory_system/README.md` - Read full README
- Check for memory schema docs, playbook format specs

### 1.2 Code Investigation
- Launch code investigation agent to understand:
  - Memory storage format (JSON/YAML playbooks)
  - How it integrates with CASS for session mining
  - Playbook generation algorithm
  - Procedural memory patterns (triggers, responses)
  - /cm skill integration in Claude Code

### 1.3 CLI Command Verification
```bash
# Verify each documented command works
cm --help 2>&1 | head -20
cm recall --help 2>&1 | head -10
cm playbook --help 2>&1 | head -10

# Test actual functionality
cm list 2>&1 | head -5
```

### 1.4 External Context Search
- `/xf search 'cass memory OR cm OR procedural memory'` - Twitter archive
- `cass search 'cm memory playbook' --robot --limit 10` - Past sessions

### 1.5 Project State Review
- Check beads in /dp/cass_memory_system/.beads/
- Review recent commits: `cd /dp/cass_memory_system && git log --oneline -20`

## Phase 2: Analysis (SYNTHESIZE UNDERSTANDING)

### 2.1 Core Capabilities Verification
Document findings for each area:
- [ ] Memory storage format (file structure, schema)
- [ ] CASS integration mechanism (how it queries sessions)
- [ ] Playbook structure and generation (template format)
- [ ] Session recall capabilities (search, filter)
- [ ] Practical memory workflows

### 2.2 Synergy Verification
Cross-reference these tools actually integrate:
- [ ] cass - primary dependency for session mining
- [ ] mail - message integration
- [ ] ntm - multi-agent memory sharing

### 2.3 Claim Verification
```bash
# Verify memory storage location
ls -la ~/.local/share/cm/ 2>/dev/null || echo "No memory data yet"

# Verify playbook format
find ~/.local/share/cm -name "*.yaml" -o -name "*.json" 2>/dev/null | head -5
```

## Phase 3: Revision (UPDATE DESCRIPTIONS)

### 3.1 Update apps/web/lib/flywheel.ts
Update cm entry with VERIFIED information:
- `tagline`: Accurate one-liner from README
- `description`: Memory storage and retrieval
- `deepDescription`: How it builds playbooks from cass
- `features`: Verified memory capabilities
- `cliCommands`: Only commands that actually work
- `connectsTo`: Only verified integrations (must include cass)

### 3.2 Update apps/web/lib/tldr-content.ts
Update TldrFlywheelTool entry with:
- `briefDescription`: Technical summary
- `bulletPoints`: Verified capabilities
- `synergyExamples`: Working integration examples

## Phase 4: Testing (VERIFY CHANGES)

### 4.1 TypeScript Compilation
```bash
cd apps/web && npx tsc --noEmit 2>&1 | head -20
```

### 4.2 Unit Tests
```bash
# Test cm entry structure
node -e "
const { flywheelTools } = require('./lib/flywheel');
const cm = flywheelTools.find(t => t.id === 'cm');
console.log('Testing cm entry...');
console.assert(cm, 'cm entry exists');
console.assert(cm.features?.length > 0, 'has features');
console.assert(cm.connectsTo?.includes('cass'), 'connects to cass');
console.log('All assertions passed');
"
```

### 4.3 E2E Test: Memory Workflow
```bash
#\!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail
LOG=/tmp/cm-e2e-$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S).log

echo "=== CM E2E Test ===" | tee $LOG
echo "Started: $(date)" | tee -a $LOG

# Test 1: List memories
echo "Test 1: List memories..." | tee -a $LOG
cm list 2>&1 | tee -a $LOG

# Test 2: Recall capability
echo "Test 2: Recall test..." | tee -a $LOG
cm recall "testing" --limit 3 2>&1 | tee -a $LOG || echo "No matches (OK)"

# Test 3: Playbook generation (dry run if available)
echo "Test 3: Playbook check..." | tee -a $LOG
cm playbook --help 2>&1 | head -5 | tee -a $LOG

echo "=== All Tests Passed ===" | tee -a $LOG
echo "Log: $LOG"
```

### 4.4 Content Diff Verification
```bash
echo "=== Changes Made ==="
diff $SNAPSHOT_DIR/flywheel.ts.before apps/web/lib/flywheel.ts || true
diff $SNAPSHOT_DIR/tldr-content.ts.before apps/web/lib/tldr-content.ts || true
```

## Phase 5: Completion (FINALIZE)

### 5.1 Rollback Procedure (if tests fail)
```bash
cp $SNAPSHOT_DIR/flywheel.ts.before apps/web/lib/flywheel.ts
cp $SNAPSHOT_DIR/tldr-content.ts.before apps/web/lib/tldr-content.ts
echo "Rolled back to pre-exploration state"
```

### 5.2 Sync Changes
```bash
br update bd-w8iz --status done
br sync --flush-only
```

### 5.3 Final Verification
- [ ] All tests pass
- [ ] TypeScript compiles without errors
- [ ] Content matches verified tool capabilities
- [ ] Synergies are reciprocal (cm->cass and cass->cm)
- [ ] No broken links or references","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-26T20:01:14.505672108Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-26T21:31:37.021485698Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-zhdi","title":"Shell tab completion for acfs command","description":"## Overview
Add shell tab completion for the `acfs` command (bash and zsh).

## Current Problem
- Users must remember all subcommands
- No discovery mechanism for available options
- Typos in command names cause errors

## Proposed Completion
```bash
$ acfs <TAB>
doctor    install   newproj   status    update

$ acfs doctor --<TAB>
--fix     --json    --quiet   --verbose

$ acfs install <TAB>
all       core      dev       shell     # module names from manifest
```

## Implementation Details
1. Generate completions from manifest.yaml at install time
2. Support both bash and zsh
3. Install to standard locations:
   - Bash: ~/.local/share/bash-completion/completions/acfs
   - Zsh: ~/.local/share/zsh/site-functions/_acfs
4. Source automatically via shell rc files

## Completion Sources
- Static: command names (doctor, install, update, etc.)
- Dynamic: module names from manifest.yaml
- Dynamic: tool names for granular install

## Installation
```bash
# Added to install.sh
acfs_install_completions() {
  local shell="$1"
  case "$shell" in
    bash) install_bash_completions ;;
    zsh)  install_zsh_completions ;;
  esac
}
```

## Test Plan
- [ ] Test bash completion for all commands
- [ ] Test zsh completion for all commands
- [ ] Test dynamic module name completion
- [ ] Test completion after fresh install
- [ ] Test update preserves completions

## Files to Create
- scripts/completions/acfs.bash
- scripts/completions/_acfs (zsh)
- Modify: scripts/install.sh (install completions)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:01:32.204228108Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T23:06:38.397635001Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-zhdi","depends_on_id":"bd-3y1n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:03:36.006705246Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
